message:
fix(tests): fail loud on null source_path/line_number in jogco hit_keys (deep self-review)

Self-review lens (a) — assertion tightness — applied to the
tests/e2e_lexical_fail_open.rs::default_hybrid_hit_list_equals_explicit_lexical_when_semantic_absent
test from commit 7066270e.

The `hit_keys` helper used `.unwrap_or("")` for source_path and
`.unwrap_or(-1)` for line_number. If a future regression in the
search result envelope emitted null/missing source_path or
line_number in BOTH the default-mode and --mode lexical paths, both
hit lists would collapse to identical `("", -1)` tuples and the
equivalence assertion would trivially pass — hollowing out the guard
the helper exists to enforce. A parallel regression that emitted
well-formed hits for only one of the two modes would also slip if
the other mode's hits all defaulted the same way.

Switched both field extractions to panicking closures that print the
offending hit JSON on failure. The common case (well-formed DB-
sourced hits, both modes) is unaffected; the regression path now
fails loud.

Verified locally:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p3 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_lexical_fail_open
  -> 2 passed in 2.38s (existing test + jogco equivalence test)

Deep review summary of the remaining pane-3 commits (review of
31fa0fe1 2hh1s, a2d949dd 8qet9, 6c33e2fb ev4f7, 515e43d7 0a8y3):
all clean under the four lenses (assertion tightness, /tmp safety via
TempDir RAII, subprocess cleanup via .output() + TempDir drop, stderr
included-in-error-message rather than asserted-clean). The jogco
hit_keys defaulting was the sole real tightness gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
